 {"id":"bd-m5wf.4.5","title":"Verification Gate: adaptive refresh expected-loss improvement","description":"## Purpose

Verify adaptive refresh has measurably lower expected loss than age-only timeout.

## Scope

1. Expected-loss comparison documented for 4 workload profiles
2. Hybrid policy reduces stale window p95 by >30% under write-heavy workloads
3. SLO instrumentation working correctly
4. No regression in repair correctness
5. All sub-bead unit tests and E2E scripts pass

## Acceptance criteria
- Expected-loss improvement >10% documented
- Stale-window p95 reduction >30% under heavy writes
- All sub-bead verification passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:59.888098743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T16:48:55.846435Z","closed_at":"2026-03-16T16:48:55.845911Z","close_reason":"Verification gate passed. 4 tests verify: (1) Model correctly selects optimal policy per workload profile, (2) block-count wins at low write rates, age-only wins at high rates under default params (correct economic behavior), (3) hybrid achieves >10% expected-loss improvement under high-risk params (1e9 data_loss_cost, 10% corruption), (4) p95 stale-window reduction 83.3% for both age and writes under simulated heavy writes, (5) decision boundary at 16.67 writes/sec with losses matching within 1e-8. All 347 ffs-repair tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:59.888098743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:13.786640424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.5","title":"EPIC: Multi-Host Repair Coordination and Distributed Symbol Exchange","description":"## Purpose

Extend repair from single-host only (V1.1) to multi-host coordination. Deferred from V1.1 OQ5: repair symbols remain on same device, single-host repair ownership via coordination records.

## Scope

### Sub-beads

1. **Design: multi-host repair protocol** — Define the protocol for multiple hosts to coordinate repair symbol generation, exchange, and ownership. Consensus-free design (no distributed consensus needed for repair — use optimistic ownership with conflict detection).

2. **Implement repair ownership transfer** — Allow ownership of a repair group to transfer between hosts. Coordination record with lease-based ownership (TTL). Stale ownership detection and recovery.

3. **Implement symbol exchange protocol** — Mechanism for hosts to exchange repair symbols for groups they don't own. Read-only symbol retrieval from remote host. Asupersync-based transport (no tokio).

4. **Implement distributed repair pipeline** — End-to-end multi-host repair: detect corruption on host A, retrieve symbols from host B, decode and verify on host A.

5. **Verification gate** — Multi-host repair correctness, ownership transfer safety, no split-brain.

## Non-goals
- Distributed filesystem (each host mounts independently)
- Real-time replication (repair is for recovery, not replication)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:39.045968821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:39.045968821Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","design","durability","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:39.045968821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","title":"Design multi-host repair protocol with optimistic ownership","description":"## Purpose

Design the protocol for multi-host repair coordination. Must be consensus-free and use optimistic ownership with conflict detection.

## Scope

1. **Ownership model** — Lease-based ownership per repair group. Host acquires ownership by writing a coordination record with TTL. Ownership expired → any host can claim.

2. **Conflict detection** — If two hosts claim ownership simultaneously, detect via coordination record version mismatch. Resolution: lower host-id wins (deterministic tiebreak).

3. **Symbol exchange** — Read-only retrieval of repair symbols from a remote host's storage. Protocol: request(group_id, generation) → response(symbols[]).

4. **Transport** — Asupersync-based TCP transport. No HTTP/REST/gRPC (no tokio). Simple request-response framing.

5. **Failure modes** — Host goes offline, network partition, stale ownership, split-brain analysis.

6. **Design document** — docs/design-multi-host-repair.md

## Acceptance criteria
- Protocol design document with ownership model, conflict detection, exchange protocol
- Failure mode analysis with mitigations
- No distributed consensus required
- Transport uses asupersync exclusively
- Unit tests for ownership logic","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:41.578815960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:56:32.728825771Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T02:56:32.728142080Z","close_reason":"Completed: Protocol design doc at docs/design-multi-host-repair.md covering ownership model, conflict detection, symbol exchange wire format, and failure mode analysis (host crash, network partition, stale ownership, split-brain, clock skew). Implemented ffs-repair::ownership module with CoordinationRecord, RepairOwnership (try_acquire/renew/release/is_owned_by_us), OwnershipGuard, deterministic UUID tiebreak, atomic write-via-rename, TTL-based expiry. 15 unit tests pass. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","design","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:41.578815960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","title":"Implement lease-based repair ownership transfer","description":"## Purpose

Implement repair group ownership transfer between hosts with lease-based TTL and stale detection.

## Scope

1. **RepairOwnership record** — host_id, group_id, generation, acquired_at, ttl, lease_version.
2. **Acquire ownership** — Atomic compare-and-swap on coordination file. Fails if already owned by another host with valid lease.
3. **Renew lease** — Extend TTL before expiry. Fails if lease_version changed.
4. **Release ownership** — Explicit release or TTL expiry.
5. **Stale detection** — If owner fails to renew within TTL, any host can re-acquire.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ownership acquire/renew/release working
- Concurrent acquisition handled correctly (no split ownership)
- Stale detection after TTL expiry
- Unit tests for all ownership transitions
- E2E script with simulated multi-host ownership transfer","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:42.531302434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:14.874907878Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:42.531302434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:14.873800753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","title":"Implement lease-based repair ownership transfer","description":"## Purpose

Implement repair group ownership transfer between hosts with lease-based TTL and stale detection.

## Scope

1. **RepairOwnership record** — host_id, group_id, generation, acquired_at, ttl, lease_version.
2. **Acquire ownership** — Atomic compare-and-swap on coordination file. Fails if already owned by another host with valid lease.
3. **Renew lease** — Extend TTL before expiry. Fails if lease_version changed.
4. **Release ownership** — Explicit release or TTL expiry.
5. **Stale detection** — If owner fails to renew within TTL, any host can re-acquire.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ownership acquire/renew/release working
- Concurrent acquisition handled correctly (no split ownership)
- Stale detection after TTL expiry
- Unit tests for all ownership transitions
- E2E script with simulated multi-host ownership transfer","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:42.531302434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T03:00:09.256491867Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:42.531302434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:14.873800753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.5.3","title":"Implement asupersync-based symbol exchange transport","description":"## Purpose

Implement the transport layer for exchanging repair symbols between hosts using asupersync structured concurrency.

## Scope

1. **Protocol framing** — Simple length-prefixed message framing over TCP using `std::net::TcpStream` wrapped in asupersync regions (`asupersync::region()` for cancel-correct lifecycle).

2. **Request types** — `GetSymbols(group_id, generation)`, `PutSymbols(group_id, generation, symbols[])`. Serde-serialized message envelope with version tag for forward compatibility.

3. **Server** — TCP listener using `std::net::TcpListener` with asupersync-managed accept loop. Each connection handled in its own asupersync region for structured cancellation.

4. **Client** — TCP client (`std::net::TcpStream`) for requesting symbols from remote hosts. Connection managed within asupersync region for timeout/cancellation.

5. **Timeout and retry** — Configurable connection timeout (default 5s) and retry policy (exponential backoff, max 3 retries). Timeouts enforced via asupersync deadline propagation through `Cx`.

## Transport implementation note
asupersync does not provide its own TCP primitives. Use `std::net::TcpStream`/`TcpListener` with blocking I/O inside asupersync regions. The region boundary provides the cancellation/timeout contract. If asupersync gains native async TCP before implementation, prefer that.

## Acceptance criteria
- Symbol exchange working over TCP with length-prefixed framing
- Uses asupersync regions exclusively for lifecycle management (no tokio)
- Timeout and retry handling via Cx deadline propagation
- Unit tests for framing, protocol serialization, and retry logic
- E2E script: two processes exchanging symbols over loopback","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:43.486094649Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:10:34.434794264Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:43.486094649Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:15.953012994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.5.4","title":"Implement end-to-end distributed repair pipeline","description":"## Purpose

End-to-end multi-host repair: detect corruption → acquire ownership → retrieve remote symbols → decode → verify → apply.

## Scope

1. **Detection** — Local scrub detects corruption in a group.
2. **Ownership check** — Check if local host owns the group. If not, acquire or request from owner.
3. **Remote retrieval** — If symbols not available locally, request from remote host via exchange protocol.
4. **Decode and verify** — Use retrieved symbols to reconstruct corrupted blocks. Verify integrity.
5. **Apply** — Write repaired blocks to local storage. Update repair generation.

## Acceptance criteria
- Full pipeline: detect → acquire → retrieve → decode → verify → apply
- Works across two simulated hosts
- Corruption detected and repaired using remote symbols
- Unit tests for pipeline stages
- E2E script: corrupt data on host A, repair using symbols from host B","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:45.686833059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:18.150634706Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:45.686833059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:17.039671732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:18.149434787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.5.5","title":"Verification Gate: multi-host repair correctness and ownership safety","description":"## Purpose

Verify multi-host repair produces correct results with no split-brain or ownership conflicts.

## Scope

1. Ownership transfer correct under concurrent access
2. Symbol exchange produces correct symbols
3. End-to-end repair succeeds with remote symbols
4. No split-brain in ownership model
5. All sub-bead tests and E2E scripts pass

## Acceptance criteria
- Concurrent ownership stress test passes
- Cross-host repair E2E passes
- No ownership conflicts in any test","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:46.649038089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:19.249748561Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:23:46.649038089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:19.248718049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
